title: Validate in simulation before hardware
description: Exercise behavior, faults, and the safety stop in a physics simulator so the first hardware run is not the first test.
body: Steps: 1. Build a simulation with the robot model, sensors, and realistic noise/latency. 2. Run nominal behavior and confirm frames, fusion, and control work end-to-end. 3. Inject faults: dropped sensors, delayed messages, a killed node, and out-of-range commands, and confirm safe responses. 4. Test the emergency stop under each fault. 5. Note known sim-to-real gaps (unmodeled dynamics, latency) before moving to hardware at reduced authority. Rollback point: before the first real-hardware motion. Validation: nominal and fault scenarios behave safely in sim and the stop always works; passing once in sim is necessary but not sufficient, so still ramp authority on hardware carefully.
